---
title: "Ad-Hoc Revenue Data Check"
format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: cosmo
      dark:  darkly
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: full
    code-tools: true
    code-fold: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    smooth-scroll: true
    embed-resources: true
    fig-responsive: true
    fig-align: center
    fig-width: 11
    fig-height: 5
    grid:
      body-width: 1180px
      sidebar-width: 260px
---

<!--
  Static version of the revenue data check — no Shiny server required.
  All data is pre-loaded at render time; filtering runs in the browser via
  Observable JS. Deploy as a plain HTML file on Posit Connect (quarto-static).

  To move to Snowflake: replace connect_db() and the aggregation query below.
-->

```{r}
#| include: false

library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(knitr)
library(markdown)

# ojs_define() is normally injected by Quarto only when OJS cells are present.
# Source the static version so this setup chunk can call it without a stub
# OJS cell (which would embed extra bridge JS in the HTML output).
local({
  ojs_static_r <- normalizePath(file.path(
    dirname(Sys.which("quarto")), "..", "share", "rmd", "ojs_static.R"
  ), mustWork = FALSE)
  if (file.exists(ojs_static_r)) {
    source(ojs_static_r, local = globalenv())
  }
})

ROW_CAP    <- 500L
DUCKDB_PATH <- file.path(tempdir(), "revenue_demo.duckdb")

seed_duckdb <- function(path) {
  set.seed(42)
  firsts <- sort(seq(as.Date(cut(Sys.Date(), "month")), by = "-1 month",
                     length.out = 19))
  dates  <- firsts[-1] - 1

  customer_types <- c("Subscription Customer",
                      "Re-Occurring Usage Customer",
                      "Indirect Customer")
  product_lines  <- c("Sales Tax Calc", "Returns Filing",
                      "Cert Management", "Use Tax")
  sources <- c("CPQ", "NETSUITE ZAB", "EXCISE", "MYLODGE", "ZUORA INTEGRATED")

  n_subs <- 1000L
  subs <- data.frame(
    SUBSCRIPTION_KEY = sprintf("SUB%05d", seq_len(n_subs)),
    CUSTOMER_TYPE    = sample(customer_types, n_subs, replace = TRUE,
                              prob = c(.70, .20, .10)),
    PRODUCT_LINE     = sample(product_lines, n_subs, replace = TRUE),
    SOURCE           = sample(sources, n_subs, replace = TRUE,
                              prob = c(.60, .15, .10, .10, .05)),
    BASE_ARR         = round(runif(n_subs, 2000, 500000), 2),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  fact <- do.call(rbind, lapply(dates, function(d) {
    data.frame(
      REPORT_DATE      = d,
      SUBSCRIPTION_KEY = subs$SUBSCRIPTION_KEY,
      CUSTOMER_TYPE    = subs$CUSTOMER_TYPE,
      PRODUCT_LINE     = subs$PRODUCT_LINE,
      SOURCE           = subs$SOURCE,
      ARR_USD          = round(subs$BASE_ARR * runif(n_subs, 0.9, 1.1), 2),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
  fact$MRR_USD <- round(fact$ARR_USD / 12, 2)

  con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = path)
  on.exit(DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE), add = TRUE)
  DBI::dbWriteTable(con, "FACTARR", fact, overwrite = TRUE)
}

seed_duckdb(DUCKDB_PATH)

connect_db <- function() {
  DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = DUCKDB_PATH, read_only = TRUE)
}

con      <- connect_db()
all_data <- DBI::dbGetQuery(con, sprintf("
  SELECT
      REPORT_DATE
      , CUSTOMER_TYPE
      , PRODUCT_LINE
      , SOURCE
      , COUNT(DISTINCT SUBSCRIPTION_KEY)  AS SUBSCRIPTION_COUNT
      , SUM(ARR_USD)                      AS TOTAL_ARR_USD
      , SUM(MRR_USD)                      AS TOTAL_MRR_USD
  FROM FACTARR
  GROUP BY REPORT_DATE, CUSTOMER_TYPE, PRODUCT_LINE, SOURCE
  ORDER BY REPORT_DATE, TOTAL_ARR_USD DESC
  LIMIT %d
", ROW_CAP * 18L))
DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

all_data$REPORT_DATE <- as.character(all_data$REPORT_DATE)
snapshot_dates       <- sort(unique(all_data$REPORT_DATE))

sql_template <- sprintf("
SELECT
    REPORT_DATE
    , CUSTOMER_TYPE
    , PRODUCT_LINE
    , SOURCE
    , COUNT(DISTINCT SUBSCRIPTION_KEY)  AS SUBSCRIPTION_COUNT
    , SUM(ARR_USD)                      AS TOTAL_ARR_USD
    , SUM(MRR_USD)                      AS TOTAL_MRR_USD
FROM FACTARR
WHERE REPORT_DATE = (
    SELECT MAX(REPORT_DATE)
    FROM FACTARR
    WHERE REPORT_DATE <= CAST(? AS DATE)
)
GROUP BY REPORT_DATE, CUSTOMER_TYPE, PRODUCT_LINE, SOURCE
ORDER BY TOTAL_ARR_USD DESC
LIMIT %d
", ROW_CAP)

ojs_define(
  all_data       = all_data,
  snapshot_dates = snapshot_dates,
  sql_template   = sql_template,
  today_str      = as.character(Sys.Date())
)
```

## Run a report

Pick a report date (defaults to today) and click **Run now**. The query runs
at render time — clicking costs nothing and filters pre-loaded data in the
browser. The date resolves to the latest snapshot on or before it.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof selected_date = Inputs.date({
  label: "Report date",
  value: new Date(today_str + "T00:00:00"),
  max:   today_str
})

viewof run_btn = {
  const btn = html`<button class="btn btn-primary btn-lg" style="margin-top:4px">▶ Run now</button>`
  let count = 0
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    count++
    btn.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input"))
  })
  Object.defineProperty(btn, "value", { get: () => count })
  return btn
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ojs_define() passes R data frames in columnar format; transpose() converts
// to the row-oriented array that .filter() and Inputs.* expect.
all_data_rows = transpose(all_data)

localDateStr = d =>
  d == null
    ? today_str
    : d.getFullYear() + "-" +
      String(d.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, "0") + "-" +
      String(d.getDate()).padStart(2, "0")

date_str = localDateStr(selected_date)

resolved_snapshot = {
  const eligible = snapshot_dates.filter(s => s <= date_str)
  return eligible.length > 0 ? eligible[eligible.length - 1] : null
}

run_date = run_btn > 0
  ? new Date().toLocaleString("en-US", {dateStyle: "short", timeStyle: "short"})
  : ""

filtered_rows = run_btn > 0 && resolved_snapshot != null
  ? all_data_rows
      .filter(r => r.REPORT_DATE === resolved_snapshot)
      .map(r => ({...r, RUN_DATE: run_date}))
  : []
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

run_btn > 0
  ? (resolved_snapshot != null
      ? md`Returned **${filtered_rows.length}** rows for snapshot date **${resolved_snapshot}**.`
      : md`No snapshot found on or before **${date_str}**.`)
  : html`<span></span>`
```

## Output

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Results

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof row_search = Inputs.search(filtered_rows, {
  label:       "Search",
  placeholder: "Filter any column…"
})

run_btn > 0
  ? Inputs.table(row_search, {
      format: {
        TOTAL_ARR_USD: v => "$" + v.toLocaleString("en-US", {
          minimumFractionDigits: 2, maximumFractionDigits: 2
        }),
        TOTAL_MRR_USD: v => "$" + v.toLocaleString("en-US", {
          minimumFractionDigits: 2, maximumFractionDigits: 2
        })
      },
      columns: ["REPORT_DATE", "CUSTOMER_TYPE", "PRODUCT_LINE", "SOURCE",
                "SUBSCRIPTION_COUNT", "TOTAL_ARR_USD", "TOTAL_MRR_USD", "RUN_DATE"],
      width: {
        REPORT_DATE:        110,
        CUSTOMER_TYPE:      200,
        PRODUCT_LINE:       150,
        SOURCE:             150,
        SUBSCRIPTION_COUNT: 130,
        TOTAL_ARR_USD:      150,
        TOTAL_MRR_USD:      150,
        RUN_DATE:           160
      }
    })
  : html`<p><em>Click <strong>▶ Run now</strong> above to see results.</em></p>`
```

### Verified SQL

The exact query that would run against the warehouse, with the report date
filled in. Copy it into Snowflake to reproduce these numbers independently.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

resolved_sql = run_btn > 0 && resolved_snapshot != null
  ? sql_template.replace("?", `'${date_str}'`)
  : "(Click ▶ Run now to generate SQL.)"

html`<pre style="white-space: pre-wrap; font-size: 0.82em;">${resolved_sql}</pre>`
```

:::

## Export

Download the current result set as CSV. To open in Excel, use
**Data → Get Data → From Text/CSV**.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

csv_string = {
  if (filtered_rows.length === 0) return ""
  const cols = ["REPORT_DATE", "CUSTOMER_TYPE", "PRODUCT_LINE", "SOURCE",
                "SUBSCRIPTION_COUNT", "TOTAL_ARR_USD", "TOTAL_MRR_USD", "RUN_DATE"]
  const esc = v =>
    (typeof v === "string" && /[",\n]/.test(v))
      ? `"${v.replace(/"/g, '""')}"` : v
  return [
    cols.join(","),
    ...filtered_rows.map(r => cols.map(c => esc(r[c])).join(","))
  ].join("\n")
}

{
  const disabled = filtered_rows.length === 0
  const label = disabled
    ? "No data — run report first"
    : `⬇ Download CSV (${filtered_rows.length} rows)`
  const btn = html`<button
    style="padding:6px 16px;cursor:${disabled ? "not-allowed" : "pointer"}"
    ${disabled ? "disabled" : ""}>
    ${label}
  </button>`
  if (!disabled) {
    btn.onclick = () => {
      const a = Object.assign(document.createElement("a"), {
        href:     URL.createObjectURL(
                    new Blob([csv_string], {type: "text/csv"})
                  ),
        download: `revenue_check_${date_str}.csv`
      })
      a.click()
    }
  }
  return btn
}
```
